item.title: backlog edit has no --clear-ac flag to reset acceptance criteria
item.intent: Users must be able to reset AC via CLI — without --clear-ac, wrong AC entries require hand-editing JSON, violating the CLI-first design principle.
item.description: backlog edit --step has a companion --clear-steps flag; backlog edit --scope has a companion --clear-scope flag. But --ac has no --clear-ac. Once AC entries are added, the only way to remove them is to hand-edit the backlog JSON. This is inconsistent with the established pattern and violates CLI-first design. Fix: add --clear-ac to cmd_backlog_edit() that resets acceptanceCriteria to [], following the same pattern as --clear-steps.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_edit() and locate the --clear-steps case as reference (around line 3843-3846)
Add a --clear-ac case: set jq_updates with .acceptanceCriteria = [] and update update_desc
Add --clear-ac documentation to the help text in cmd_backlog_edit error message and cmd_help
Test: backlog edit BUG-003 --clear-ac (verify AC resets), then backlog edit BUG-003 --ac 'New' (verify fresh entry appears)
acceptanceCriteria: backlog edit <ID> --clear-ac --ac 'New AC' results in only the new entry present
--clear-ac appears in the backlog edit help output
